text: {"claims":[{"statement":"For each increment of 4 eggs per week, the overall cardiovascular disease risk was 6% greater.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"for each increment of 4 eggs/week, the overall CVD risk was 6% greater","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly reports a dose‑response finding that adding 4 eggs per week is associated with a 6% higher CVD risk, which supports the claim.","population":"","intervention":"increase of 4 eggs per week","comparator":"lower egg intake","outcome":"overall cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"6% greater risk per 4 eggs/week","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Overall consumption of each additional egg per day was associated with a small but significant increase in risk of cardiovascular disease of 4%.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"overall consumption of each additional egg/day was associated with a small but significant increase in risk of CVD of 4%","entailment":"The quoted text explicitly states that each extra egg per day is linked to a 4% increase in CVD risk, providing the evidence for the claim.","population":"","intervention":"each additional egg per day","comparator":"non‑consumption or lower consumption","outcome":"risk of cardiovascular disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"4% increase per additional egg/day","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Three systematic reviews and meta‑analyses looking at risk of cardiovascular disease found no significant effects of egg consumption.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"the other three SRMA looking at risk of CVD found no significant effects of egg consumption","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports that three SRMAs observed no significant impact of egg intake on CVD risk, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"risk of cardiovascular disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"no significant effects","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"The systematic review and meta‑analysis by Yang et al. reported an overall 7% increased risk of cardiovascular disease mortality with each increment of one egg per day.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"the SRMA by Yang et al., which reported an overall 7% increased risk of CVD mortality with each increment of 1 egg/day","entailment":"The quoted passage states that Yang et al.'s SRMA found a 7% higher CVD mortality risk for each additional egg per day, which underlies the claim.","population":"","intervention":"each additional egg per day","comparator":"lower egg intake","outcome":"cardiovascular disease mortality","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"7% increased risk per egg/day","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"In individuals with type 2 diabetes, high egg consumers had a 40% increased risk of cardiovascular disease.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In those with T2D, high egg consumers had 40% increased risk of CVD","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly indicates that among people with type 2 diabetes, those who consume more eggs experience a 40% higher CVD risk, supporting the claim.","population":"people with type 2 diabetes","intervention":"high egg consumption","comparator":"lower egg consumption","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"40% increased risk","modality":"associational"}]}
